seo.title: Compound Interest Calculator | Numerly
seo.description: Estimate investment growth with compounding frequency and optional additional deposits.

intro: Estimate long-term investment growth with compounding and optional deposits.
explanation: Enter the initial balance, interest rate, time period, and compounding frequency. Use the additional deposits section if you plan to add money each period.

example.input: $10,000 principal, 8% annual rate, monthly compounding, 10 years
steps: Convert annual rate to periodic rate (r/n)
Compute growth of principal over nt periods
Add annuity future value if recurring contributions are entered
example.result: Future value shows invested capital and interest growth separately.

faqs.a: It is interest earned on both principal and previously earned interest.
Yes. More frequent compounding generally increases future value.
Yes, the calculator includes recurring contribution growth.
No. Actual returns vary by market conditions and fees.

faqs.q: Can I use this for retirement planning?
faqs.a: Yes, it is useful for long-term savings and retirement projections.

instructions: Convert annual rate to periodic rate (r/n)
Compute growth of principal over nt periods
Add annuity future value if recurring contributions are entered
formulaExplanation: Enter principal, annual return, compounding frequency, contribution amount, and years. The calculator applies standard future value formulas.
edgeCases: Very long terms can significantly increase interest cost.
Zero or near-zero rates reduce compounding impact.
Always verify units (monthly vs annual) before interpreting output.
practicalTips: Compare at least two scenarios before making decisions.
Use conservative assumptions for rates and growth.
Review total cost, not just monthly amount.
